raw_title: Local Experience, National Media, and Misperceptions of the COVID-19 Pandemic
title_en: Local Experience, National Media, and Misperceptions of the COVID-19 Pandemic
title_zh: 地方经验、国家媒体与对COVID-19大流行的误解
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了人们如何形成对COVID-19大流行的误解。它关注地方经验（如个人在社区中的经历）和国家媒体（如电视新闻和报纸）如何影响人们对疫情的看法。研究发现，当地方经验与媒体报道不一致时，人们更容易产生错误的理解。这有助于解释为什么不同地区的人对疫情的看法差异很大。
plain_summary_en: This study explores how people develop misperceptions about the COVID-19 pandemic. It focuses on how local experience (such as personal encounters in one's community) and national media (like TV news and newspapers) shape people's views of the outbreak. The research finds that when local experience conflicts with media coverage, people are more likely to form incorrect understandings. This helps explain why perceptions of the pandemic vary widely across different regions.
research_direction_zh: 研究关注信息环境如何塑造公众对重大事件的认知，特别是在危机时期。它探讨了地方与国家级信息来源的互动，以及这种互动如何导致误解或分歧。这一方向有助于理解媒体效应、政治传播和公众舆论形成的过程。
research_direction_en: The research examines how information environments shape public perceptions of major events, especially during crises. It investigates the interplay between local and national sources of information and how this can lead to misperceptions or polarization. This direction contributes to understanding media effects, political communication, and the formation of public opinion.
